title: Response Status, Success Variants, Failure Taxonomy, and Problem Details · Browser and Node Client Systems · Type Forensics
summary: A browser support engineer tracing a cross-runtime client incident must isolate a plausible but incorrect result from conflicting traces, repair its cause, and retain a regression witness for response status, success variants, failure taxonomy, and problem details, producing typed response and failure classifier. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch typed response and failure classifier and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression…
objectives: Interpret informational, successful, redirection, client-error, and server-error status codes as protocol metadata. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Handle 201, 202, 204, 206, and other success responses with their location, body, and completion implications. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Separate request construction, transport, status, representation, and domain failures in a stable client contract. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Status Families
steps.instruction:
debug case — cross-runtime browser support client observes 202 without a result, 204 with no content, 206 with a range, 429 with Retry-After, and 503 problem+json for case T-4. Preserve status, transport, schema, domain, partial, and unknown-effect outcomes separately. Competency probe: Interpret informational, successful, redirection, client-error, and server-error status codes as protocol metadata. Competency probe: Represent partial results and unknown write outcomes so callers do not silently retry or declare success. For Fetch and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Partial And Ambiguous Outcomes, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: A timeout proves the server did not perform the requested operation.
